meta.notes: Phase 1 draft. HPO MUST approve user_requirements.json before BRD/SRS are treated as READY_FOR_BUILD. | Phase 2: updated requirements using answered OQs; removed blocking unknowns.

what_must_be_true: HPO explicitly approves user_requirements.json before any BRD or SRS is treated as ready.
Approval is recorded with date and hash.

business_idea.summary: Judge-ready Gut–Brain Simulator web app that demonstrates a computational pipeline: diet inputs → MODELED microbiome proxies → MODELED metabolite proxies → MODELED cognitive proxy outputs; benchmarked against de-identified teen pilot association charts (n=66); never used for training or tuning.

evidence.snippet: Modeling Diet-Driven Microbiome and Neurotransmitter Pathways

datasets are rarely integrated into a unified biological framework. This project uses a multi-stage  machine learning approach to explicitly model the biological sequence linking diet to cognition.  I
○ De-identified 2025 student dataset containing only numeric diet quality scores and  cognitive performance metrics, used solely for validation and benchmarking and  not for model training

- **Pilot Results (n=66)**: upload/select the real CSV → auto-generate charts and a clean summary table.   - **Simulator**: nutrient inputs (manual or FoodData Central lookup) → predicted microbiome s

goal.statement: Make it easy for judges to understand and trust the project’s mechanistic simulation and pilot association benchmarking within a short live demo, with reproducible exports.

primary_user.role: High school student presenter (Yana) demonstrating to STEM Showcase judges.
primary_user.context: Live demo during science fair judging; needs a stable, low-friction walkthrough.
constraints: No new human subjects; pilot dataset is de-identified and benchmark-only (association charts only; no microbiome/metabolomics measured).

problem_statement.current_state: Most gut–brain studies (and student projects) are judged as correlational; it is hard to communicate intermediate biological mechanisms and ML rigor quickly during judging.
problem_statement.desired_state: A transparent simulator dashboard that shows the multi-stage pipeline, real pilot association charts, and explicit leakage/limitation guardrails.

evidence.snippet: datasets are rarely integrated into a unified biological framework. This project uses a multi-stage  machine learning approach to explicitly model the biological sequence linking diet to cognition.  I
This approach allows for simulation of how changes in diet propagate through biological systems  and influence cognitive outcomes.

- **Pilot Results (n=66)**: upload/select the real CSV → auto-generate charts and a clean summary table.   - **Simulator**: nutrient inputs (manual or FoodData Central lookup) → predicted microbiome s

metrics.notes: cannot confirm; define measurable success metrics for the showcase (see OQ-010).
assumption; adjust if you have a different target.
assumption; adjust if you prefer a different metric.

- **Pilot Results (n=66)**: upload/select the real CSV → auto-generate charts and a clean summary table.   - **Simulator**: nutrient inputs (manual or FoodData Central lookup) → predicted microbiome s

in_scope: Pilot dataset visualization (n=66): summary stats + charts + correlations.
Simulator: diet inputs → modeled microbiome → modeled metabolites → modeled cognition.
Methods & Rigor: leakage guardrails, limitations, and disclaimers.
out_of_scope: Medical diagnosis, treatment, or personalized clinical guidance.
Collecting new human subject data (surveys, stool samples, etc.).
Live re-training of large multi-omics models in the app.

user_flows.name: Judge walkthrough
steps: Show Methods & Rigor (limitations + leakage guardrails + disclaimer).
Run a Simulator scenario with diet inputs.

user_flows.name: Pilot dataset check
steps: Validate column mapping and row count.

requirements.statement: The app must support a low-friction 'kiosk/presenter mode' suitable for live judging (no login by default).
requirements.rationale: Reduces demo friction and risk of authentication failures during judging.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the app lands on the main dashboard without authentication prompts.
Admin actions (if any) are protected separately (password or hidden toggle).
requirements.notes: Confirmed by OQ-003: kiosk/presenter mode by default; optional hidden admin controls.

requirements.statement: The app must allow users to define a diet scenario using explicit nutrient variables relevant to the project (e.g., fiber, sugar, saturated fat, omega-3/polyunsaturated fat proxy).
requirements.rationale: These inputs drive the simulator pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Diet scenario inputs include numeric controls for: fiber_g_per_day, added_sugar_g_per_day, saturated_fat_g_per_day, omega3_proxy_g_per_day.
Optional input calories_kcal_per_day can be enabled.
UI validates ranges and missing values with clear error messages.

requirements.statement: The app must run a deterministic, multi-stage simulation pipeline: diet → microbiome composition → metabolite proxies → cognition outputs.
requirements.rationale: This is the core scientific contribution and demonstration.
acceptance_criteria: The simulator runs three sequential stages and returns structured outputs for microbiome, metabolites, and cognition.
Simulation uses frozen, versioned model artifacts (JSON coefficients/trees) and is deterministic for the same (inputs + model_version_ids).
UI displays model version identifiers and run_hash for every run.
requirements.notes: Resolved by OQ-001 and OQ-002; artifacts are expected under /public/models/*.json.

requirements.statement: The app must contain a judge-readable 'Methods & Rigor' section that explains limitations, leakage guardrails, and non-diagnostic disclaimer.
requirements.rationale: Prevents overclaiming and addresses judge concerns.
acceptance_criteria: Methods & Rigor includes: 'benchmark-only pilot dataset' statement, leakage definition, and 'not medical advice / not diagnostic' disclaimer.
The text avoids 'proves' language and uses 'simulates' / 'hypothesis generator' framing.

requirements.statement: The app should include a 'Judge Walkthrough' or 'Presenter Mode' that guides a 2-minute demo.
acceptance_criteria: Presenter Mode highlights the minimal set of screens and hides advanced settings.
Presenter Mode can be reset to a known state.

requirements.statement: The app must visualize the de-identified teen pilot dataset (n=66) with real charts and tables (no synthetic data).
requirements.rationale: Provides real validation evidence supporting the project.
acceptance_criteria: App can load a bundled read-only pilot dataset and shows row count = 66.
App supports upload-at-runtime to replace/remove pilot dataset for non-demo deployments.
Pilot dataset is labeled 'benchmark-only (association charts)' and is never used to fit or tune simulator parameters.
requirements.notes: Resolved by OQ-005; ASM-006 edited with demo/public-deploy caveat.

requirements.statement: The app must compute and display basic statistics for the pilot dataset (summary stats and correlation/regression signals).
requirements.rationale: Judges expect quantitative analysis, not only visuals.
acceptance_criteria: App shows at least: mean/median for Diet Score and each cognitive metric.
App shows correlation coefficient between Diet Score and each cognitive metric.
requirements.notes: exact statistical methods can be simple; avoid overclaiming causality.

requirements.statement: The app must not collect or store personally identifiable information, and must label the pilot dataset as de-identified.
requirements.rationale: Compliance and privacy protection for teen data.
acceptance_criteria: No UI fields request names/emails/phones for pilot data.
Stored records contain numeric metrics only.

requirements.statement: The app must show model provenance: model version identifiers and a run identifier (hash) for each simulation run.
acceptance_criteria: UI displays model_version_ids used for the run (stage1/stage2/stage3).
UI displays a stable run_hash computed from normalized inputs + model_version_ids.
If the user loads a different artifact file, the model_version_id and artifact hash change and are reflected in subsequent runs/exports.
requirements.notes: Aligned with OQ-002 recommended artifact paths and provenance display.

requirements.statement: The app must generate an exportable 1-page report (PDF/HTML) containing inputs, outputs, pilot validation visuals, and key disclaimers.
requirements.rationale: Judges value a take-home artifact and it reinforces reproducibility.
acceptance_criteria: Export includes: diet inputs, stage outputs, pilot charts/tables, leakage guardrails, disclaimers, and run_id/model_version.
Export generation succeeds without external tooling beyond the app deployment.
requirements.notes: Export should include inputs/outputs, pilot visuals, disclaimers, and run provenance. Export format still open (OQ-007).

requirements.statement: The app should provide evidence references for each simulator module (e.g., SCFAs and tryptophan metabolites) in a judge-friendly way.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence is presented as citations/links or a curated evidence panel.
Evidence claims are phrased as 'supports plausibility' not 'proves'.

requirements.statement: All microbiome/metabolite outputs shown for teens must be labeled as modeled proxy variables, not measured biomarkers.
requirements.rationale: The pilot dataset does not contain microbiome/metabolite measurements; avoiding misrepresentation is critical.
acceptance_criteria: UI uses labels such as 'simulated' / 'estimated' / 'proxy'.
Methods & Rigor states that teens have no stool/metabolite measurements.
requirements.notes: Confirmed by OQ-006: outputs shown for teens are modeled/proxy variables; pilot has no microbiome/metabolite measurements.

requirements.statement: The app should meet basic performance targets suitable for live demos (fast load and fast simulation).
requirements.rationale: Reduces demo risk under poor network or device constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Cold start loads core UI in under 3 seconds on a typical laptop.
A single simulation run completes in under 500 ms once loaded.
requirements.notes: targets are assumptions; confirm or adjust via OQ-010.

requirements.statement: The app should support side-by-side comparison of two diet scenarios and their predicted outputs.
requirements.rationale: Improves interpretability and demonstrates dose-response behavior.
acceptance_criteria: User can save two scenarios and compare outputs on one screen.
Export can include the comparison.
requirements.notes: may be deferred if schedule is tight.

requirements.statement: The app must include an in-app Help/Docs screen that displays all submitted foundation documents and reference snapshots in read-only form, rendering JSON/CSV/MD/TXT in the browser while offline.
requirements.rationale: Improves transparency and judge trust by making the submitted packet and engineering artifacts available inside the demo app.
acceptance_criteria: Help/Docs is accessible from every screen via a consistent top-bar button.
Docs list is complete (driven by docs_index.json) and opens documents without network access.
JSON renders with stable formatting; CSV renders as a table preview; MD renders as Markdown; TXT renders as text.
requirements.notes: Read-only. No PHI. No new scientific claims; presentation-only.

unknowns.notes: Blocking questions answered. Remaining open questions are non-blocking.
